title: Incorrect simulation of flip-flop after latch
body:
There seem to be some issues when simulating a latch that feeds a flop that samples the signal on the same edge the latch becomes transparent. The flip-flop seems to be sampling the signal on the same edge it starts to be generated, and I would expect it to sample the previous value. The issue seems to be present in both verilator `5.016` and `5.018`.

Waves example: flop_ff follows flop_n. I would expect it to show the value on the next cycle.
![waves](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/assets/78434679/3c2ae7ab-69af-4c73-a252-7bd3343e1aae)

I have attached a [small testcase](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/files/13251859/test.txt) that reproduces the issue. It simulates as expected on VCS & Questa.

I'm not entirely sure what is the correct behavior for this. If the line with the `always_comb` is replaced by an assignment with no addition logic, the test fails on all simulators.
